# Software Licence Agreement — Contract K001

**Effective Date:** 1 April 2025

**Parties:**

This Software Licence Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 1 April 2025 by and between:

**Meridian Holdings Ltd**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registration number 12847391, whose registered office is at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the Buyer"); and

**Nexus

e is at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the Buyer"); and

**Nexus Procurement PLC**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registration number 09234156, whose registered office is at 12 Victoria Road, Manchester, M1 2WN (hereinafter "the Supplier").

Together the Buyer and the Supplier are referred to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

The Buyer requires a comprehensi

erred to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

The Buyer requires a comprehensive enterprise resource planning system to consolidate its financial management, purchase order processing, and operations reporting functions across its UK business units. Following a competitive tender process, the Buyer has selected the Supplier on the basis of functionality, total cost of ownership, and implementati

Following a competitive tender process, the Buyer has selected the Supplier on the basis of functionality, total cost of ownership, and implementation track record.

The Supplier has agreed to provide the Enterprise Resource Planning Suite, a fully integrated software platform encompassing accounts payable, purchase order management, inventory control, and financial reporting modules. This Agreeme

der management, inventory control, and financial reporting modules. This Agreement sets out the terms under which the Supplier will license and implement that system.

## Scope of Supply

The Supplier shall deliver one (1) perpetual, non-exclusive, non-transferable licence for the Enterprise Resource Planning Suite. The licence entitles Meridian Holdings Ltd to deploy the software across all UK-registered business units and subsidiaries for the duration of the licence term.

The licence entitles Meridian Holdings Ltd to deploy the software across all UK-registered business units and subsidiaries for the duration of the licence term. The system shall be installed on server infrastructure owned and operated by the Buyer at its London premises.

Implementation services shall be delivered within sixteen (16) weeks of the Effective Date. The Supplier shall allocate up to t

The system shall be installed on server infrastructure owned and operated by the Buyer at its London premises.

Implementation services shall be delivered within sixteen (16) weeks of the Effective Date. The Supplier shall allocate up to twenty (20) consultant days of on-site support, including project management, configuration, data migration, and end-user training sessions. The Supplier shall pr

The Supplier shall allocate up to twenty (20) consultant days of on-site support, including project management, configuration, data migration, and end-user training sessions. The Supplier shall provide Meridian Holdings Ltd with monthly progress reports throughout the implementation phase.

The Buyer's nominated contract manager for this engagement is James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer of Meridian Holdings Ltd.

The Supplier shall provide Meridian Holdings Ltd with monthly progress reports throughout the implementation phase.

The Buyer's nominated contract manager for this engagement is James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer of Meridian Holdings Ltd. James Blackwood holds authority to approve delivery milestones, authorise scope changes in writing, and formally accept the delivered system on behalf of the Buyer.

James Blackwood holds authority to approve delivery milestones, authorise scope changes in writing, and formally accept the delivered system on behalf of the Buyer. The Supplier's nominated contract lead is Sarah Chen, Head of Contracts at Nexus Procurement PLC. Sarah Chen is responsible for contract compliance, milestone certification, invoice submission, and escalation management throughout the

Sarah Chen is responsible for contract compliance, milestone certification, invoice submission, and escalation management throughout the term.

## Payment Terms

The total licence fee for the Enterprise Resource Planning Suite is eighty-five thousand pounds sterling (£85,000.00), exclusive of VAT applicable at the prevailing rate. The fee is structured in three tranches as follows:

- **First tran

ailing rate. The fee is structured in three tranches as follows:

- **First tranche:** thirty per cent (£25,500.00), due within fourteen (14) calendar days of the execution date of this Agreement.
- **Second tranche:** forty per cent (£34,000.00), due within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Buyer's written sign-off on the User Acceptance Testing phase.
- **Third tranche:** thirty per cent (£25,5

The fee is structured in three tranches as follows:

- **First tranche:** thirty per cent (£25,500.00), due within fourteen (14) calendar days of the execution date of this Agreement.
- **Second tranche:** forty per cent (£34,000.00), due within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Buyer's written sign-off on the User Acceptance Testing phase.
- **Third tranche:** thirty per cent (£25,500.00), due s

ceptance Testing phase.
- **Third tranche:** thirty per cent (£25,500.00), due sixty (60) calendar days after the confirmed go-live date, subject to no material defects remaining unresolved.

All invoices shall be addressed to the accounts payable function of Meridian Holdings Ltd and must reference the contract number K001 on their face. Payments shall be made by BACS transfer to the bank account notified by Nexus Procurement PLC in writing.

Payments shall be made by BACS transfer to the bank account notified by Nexus Procurement PLC in writing. Any invoice not disputed within ten (10) business days of receipt shall be deemed accepted. Overdue amounts shall accrue interest at four per cent (4%) per annum above the Bank of England base rate, compounding monthly. The Supplier reserves the right to suspend ongoing support services if any

Overdue amounts shall accrue interest at four per cent (4%) per annum above the Bank of England base rate, compounding monthly. The Supplier reserves the right to suspend ongoing support services if any undisputed invoice remains unpaid for more than thirty (30) calendar days after its due date.

## Confidentiality

Each Party acknowledges that in the course of performing its obligations under thi

The Supplier reserves the right to suspend ongoing support services if any undisputed invoice remains unpaid for more than thirty (30) calendar days after its due date.

## Confidentiality

Each Party acknowledges that in the course of performing its obligations under this Agreement it may receive or access Confidential Information belonging to the other. Confidential Information includes, without

l Information belonging to the other. Confidential Information includes, without limitation, pricing schedules, system architecture documentation, business process specifications, source code, financial data, and personal data processed through the Enterprise Resource Planning Suite.

Neither Party shall disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the

Confidential Information includes, without limitation, pricing schedules, system architecture documentation, business process specifications, source code, financial data, and personal data processed through the Enterprise Resource Planning Suite.

Neither Party shall disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party, except where disclos

without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party, except where disclosure is required by law or by order of a competent court or regulatory authority. Each Party shall apply no less than the same standard of care to protect the other's Confidential Information as it applies to its own confidential material, and in no event less than reasonable professional care. This obligation shall sur

Each Party shall apply no less than the same standard of care to protect the other's Confidential Information as it applies to its own confidential material, and in no event less than reasonable professional care. This obligation shall survive termination or expiry of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.

## Liability

The Supplier's total aggregate liability to the Buyer under or in con

This obligation shall survive termination or expiry of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.

## Liability

The Supplier's total aggregate liability to the Buyer under or in connection with this Agreement, whether arising in contract, tort (including negligence), breach of statutory duty, or otherwise, shall not exceed one hundred per cent (100%) of the total fees paid by Meridian Holding

ot exceed one hundred per cent (100%) of the total fees paid by Meridian Holdings Ltd to Nexus Procurement PLC under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

Neither Party shall be liable to the other for indirect or consequential loss, loss of profit, loss of business opportunity, loss of anticipated revenue, or loss of goodwill, even if that Party had been advised of the possibility of such losses.

oodwill, even if that Party had been advised of the possibility of such losses. Nothing in this Agreement excludes or limits either Party's liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, for fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation, or for any liability that cannot lawfully be limited or excluded.

## Termination

Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving ninety (90) calendar

Nothing in this Agreement excludes or limits either Party's liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, for fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation, or for any liability that cannot lawfully be limited or excluded.

## Termination

Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving ninety (90) calendar days' written notice to the other Party at any time after the first anniversary of the Effective Date.

the other Party at any time after the first anniversary of the Effective Date. Either Party may also terminate immediately on written notice if the other commits a material breach of any term of this Agreement and, where the breach is capable of remedy, fails to remedy it within thirty (30) calendar days of receiving written notice specifying the breach in reasonable detail.

Meridian Holdings Lt

Either Party may also terminate immediately on written notice if the other commits a material breach of any term of this Agreement and, where the breach is capable of remedy, fails to remedy it within thirty (30) calendar days of receiving written notice specifying the breach in reasonable detail.

Meridian Holdings Ltd may terminate with immediate effect if Nexus Procurement PLC becomes insolvent

d may terminate with immediate effect if Nexus Procurement PLC becomes insolvent, enters administration, is subject to a winding-up petition, or makes any composition or arrangement with its creditors generally. Upon termination for any reason, the Buyer shall promptly cease using the licensed software and shall, at the Supplier's election, return or securely destroy all copies of the Enterprise R

Upon termination for any reason, the Buyer shall promptly cease using the licensed software and shall, at the Supplier's election, return or securely destroy all copies of the Enterprise Resource Planning Suite and associated documentation, providing written certification of destruction within ten (10) business days. Any prepaid licence fees attributable to periods falling after the effective date

Any prepaid licence fees attributable to periods falling after the effective date of termination shall be refunded to Meridian Holdings Ltd on a pro-rata basis within thirty (30) calendar days.

## Governing Law

This Agreement, and any dispute or claim (including non-contractual disputes or claims) arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.

be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. Each Party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales for the resolution of any such dispute or claim.

---

**Signed for and on behalf of Meridian Holdings Ltd:**

James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer

Signature: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_  Date: 1 April 2025

James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer

Sarah Chen, Head of

# Cloud Migration Consultancy Agreement — Contract K002

**Effective Date:** 15 May 2025

**Parties:**

This Cloud Migration Consultancy Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 15 May 2025 by and between:

**Meridian Holdings Ltd**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registration number 12847391, whose registered office is at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the C

ose registered office is at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the Client"); and

**Hartwell Solutions Ltd**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registration number 11502847, whose registered office is at 78 High Street, Birmingham, B1 1BB (hereinafter "the Consultant").

Together the Client and the Consultant are referred to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

Meridia

nd the Consultant are referred to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

Meridian Holdings Ltd operates a portfolio of UK business units that rely on a mixture of on-premises server infrastructure and legacy virtualisation platforms established over a period of fifteen years. As part of a multi-year technology transformation programme, the Client has determined that migrating selected workloads to

As part of a multi-year technology transformation programme, the Client has determined that migrating selected workloads to a private cloud environment will reduce operational expenditure, improve resilience, and create a more scalable foundation for future enterprise applications.

Following a structured market assessment and a competitive shortlisting exercise, the Client selected Hartwell Solut

ment and a competitive shortlisting exercise, the Client selected Hartwell Solutions Ltd on the basis of the Consultant's demonstrable experience in enterprise infrastructure migration, its Birmingham-based delivery team, and its methodology for phased workload transition with minimal disruption to live operations.

## Scope of Services

Hartwell Solutions Ltd shall provide Cloud Migration Consultancy services to Meridian Holdings Ltd under this Agreement.

d Migration Consultancy services to Meridian Holdings Ltd under this Agreement. The engagement shall proceed in three phases: discovery and assessment, migration planning, and supervised migration execution.

During the discovery and assessment phase, the Consultant's team shall conduct a full audit of the Client's existing server estate, map application dependencies, identify migration blockers,

The engagement shall proceed in three phases: discovery and assessment, migration planning, and supervised migration execution.

During the discovery and assessment phase, the Consultant's team shall conduct a full audit of the Client's existing server estate, map application dependencies, identify migration blockers, and produce a written Infrastructure Assessment Report within four weeks of the Effective Date.

itten Infrastructure Assessment Report within four weeks of the Effective Date. During the migration planning phase, the Consultant shall develop a detailed workload migration schedule, define rollback procedures for each workload, and present the Migration Runbook to the Client for approval.

During the migration execution phase, the Consultant shall provide hands-on technical support for the mig

During the migration planning phase, the Consultant shall develop a detailed workload migration schedule, define rollback procedures for each workload, and present the Migration Runbook to the Client for approval.

During the migration execution phase, the Consultant shall provide hands-on technical support for the migration of up to forty virtual machine workloads, including pre-migration testing, cutover coordination, and post-migration validation.

ing pre-migration testing, cutover coordination, and post-migration validation. The Consultant shall assign a minimum of one senior consultant to each migration window.

The Consultant's nominated project lead for this engagement is Oliver Hartley, Senior Consultant at Hartwell Solutions Ltd. Oliver Hartley is responsible for day-to-day delivery management, milestone sign-off, resource scheduling, and escalation of technical risks to the Client.

Oliver Hartley is responsible for day-to-day delivery management, milestone sign-off, resource scheduling, and escalation of technical risks to the Client. The Client's contract manager is James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer of Meridian Holdings Ltd. James Blackwood holds authority to approve the Migration Runbook, authorise scope adjustments in writing, and certify the completion of each p

James Blackwood holds authority to approve the Migration Runbook, authorise scope adjustments in writing, and certify the completion of each phase on behalf of the Client.

## Payment Terms

The Cloud Migration Consultancy services shall be charged at a daily rate of one thousand two hundred and fifty pounds sterling (£1,250.00 per day), exclusive of VAT. The total contracted volume is eighty cons

£1,250.00 per day), exclusive of VAT. The total contracted volume is eighty consultant days, giving a maximum contract value of one hundred thousand pounds sterling (£100,000.00) exclusive of VAT.

Invoices shall be raised by Hartwell Solutions Ltd at the end of each calendar month, covering confirmed consultant days delivered in that month. Each invoice must quote contract reference K002 and be addressed to the accounts payable function of Meridian Holdings Ltd.

Each invoice must quote contract reference K002 and be addressed to the accounts payable function of Meridian Holdings Ltd. Payment is due within thirty (30) calendar days of receipt of a valid invoice. Overdue amounts shall accrue interest at three per cent (3%) per annum above the Bank of England base rate. Any invoice not formally disputed within ten (10) business days of receipt shall be deeme

Any invoice not formally disputed within ten (10) business days of receipt shall be deemed accepted.

The Consultant shall maintain time records for all consultant days delivered under this Agreement and shall make those records available to the Client upon request within five business days.

## Intellectual Property

All deliverables produced solely for the Client under this Agreement, including

All deliverables produced solely for the Client under this Agreement, including the Infrastructure Assessment Report, the Migration Runbook, and any bespoke configuration scripts or documentation, shall vest in Meridian Holdings Ltd upon full payment of the associated fees. Pre-existing intellectual property belonging to Hartwell Solutions Ltd, including proprietary methodology frameworks, toolset

Pre-existing intellectual property belonging to Hartwell Solutions Ltd, including proprietary methodology frameworks, toolsets, and assessment templates used in the delivery of the services, shall remain the exclusive property of the Consultant. The Client is granted a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use such pre-existing materials solely to the extent necessary to maintain and operate the

The Client is granted a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use such pre-existing materials solely to the extent necessary to maintain and operate the migrated infrastructure following completion of the engagement.

## Confidentiality

Each Party shall keep confidential all information received from the other Party that is designated as confidential or that a reasonable person would recognise as confidential given its nature and the circumstances of disclosure.

recognise as confidential given its nature and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential information under this Agreement includes, without limitation, the Client's infrastructure topology, security configurations, vendor contracts, business plans, and financial data, as well as the Consultant's proprietary methodologies, pricing structures, and client references. Neither Party shall disclose

Confidential information under this Agreement includes, without limitation, the Client's infrastructure topology, security configurations, vendor contracts, business plans, and financial data, as well as the Consultant's proprietary methodologies, pricing structures, and client references. Neither Party shall disclose such information to any third party without prior written consent, except where required by law or court order.

Neither Party shall disclose such information to any third party without prior written consent, except where required by law or court order. This obligation shall continue for five (5) years following the termination or expiry of this Agreement.

## Liability

The Consultant's aggregate liability to the Client for any and all claims arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall not exce

This obligation shall continue for five (5) years following the termination or expiry of this Agreement.

## Liability

The Consultant's aggregate liability to the Client for any and all claims arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by Meridian Holdings Ltd to Hartwell Solutions Ltd in the six (6) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

in the six (6) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim. Neither Party shall be liable for indirect or consequential losses, loss of data, loss of revenue, or loss of anticipated savings. Nothing in this Agreement limits either Party's liability for fraud, death, or personal injury caused by negligence, or for any other liability that cannot be excluded by law.

## Terminati

Nothing in this Agreement limits either Party's liability for fraud, death, or personal injury caused by negligence, or for any other liability that cannot be excluded by law.

## Termination

Either Party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) calendar days' written notice to the other at any time after completion of the discovery and assessment phase. Either Party may terminate immediately

n of the discovery and assessment phase. Either Party may terminate immediately on written notice if the other commits a material breach and, where capable of remedy, fails to remedy that breach within twenty-one (21) calendar days of written notice. Meridian Holdings Ltd may terminate with immediate effect if Hartwell Solutions Ltd enters insolvency proceedings, ceases to carry on business, or is

Meridian Holdings Ltd may terminate with immediate effect if Hartwell Solutions Ltd enters insolvency proceedings, ceases to carry on business, or is unable to provide adequately qualified personnel to fulfil its obligations.

Upon termination, the Consultant shall deliver all completed and work-in-progress deliverables to the Client within ten (10) business days. Fees for consultant days confirmed as delivered prior to the termination date shall remain payable.

Fees for consultant days confirmed as delivered prior to the termination date shall remain payable. Any fees paid in advance for services not yet delivered shall be refunded to Meridian Holdings Ltd within thirty (30) calendar days of the termination date.

---

**Signed for and on behalf of Meridian Holdings Ltd:**

James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer

Signature: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Any fees paid in advance for services not yet delivered shall be refunded to Meridian Holdings Ltd within thirty (30) calendar days of the termination date.

---

**Signed for and on behalf of Meridian Holdings Ltd:**

James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer

Signature: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_  Date: 15 May 2025

---

**Signed for and on behalf of Hartwell Solutions Ltd:**

Oliver

May 2025

Oliver Hartley, Senior Consultant

# Hardware Supply Agreement — Contract K003

**Effective Date:** 1 June 2025

**Parties:**

This Hardware Supply Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 1 June 2025 by and between:

**Meridian Holdings Ltd**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registration number 12847391, whose registered office is at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the Buyer"); and

**Albrecht

at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the Buyer"); and

**Albrecht und Partner GmbH**, a company incorporated in Germany with registration number 30482719, whose registered office is at Unter den Linden 47, Berlin, 10117, Germany (hereinafter "the Supplier").

Together the Buyer and the Supplier are referred to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

Meridian Holdings Ltd has determine

ed to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

Meridian Holdings Ltd has determined that its UK data centre and office network infrastructure requires a programme of hardware refresh and security uplift. The existing network perimeter equipment has reached end-of-support status, and a recent internal security audit identified gaps in both physical rack capacity and perimeter defence coverage across

The existing network perimeter equipment has reached end-of-support status, and a recent internal security audit identified gaps in both physical rack capacity and perimeter defence coverage across the Buyer's London and regional facilities.

Following a structured procurement exercise that included technical evaluation and a competitive price comparison, Meridian Holdings Ltd selected Albrecht un

n and a competitive price comparison, Meridian Holdings Ltd selected Albrecht und Partner GmbH as its preferred hardware supplier on the basis of product certification, delivery lead times, and post-installation warranty terms. The Supplier has confirmed that both the Network Security Appliance and the Server Rack Unit 42U are available from its standard production stock and can be delivered withi

The Supplier has confirmed that both the Network Security Appliance and the Server Rack Unit 42U are available from its standard production stock and can be delivered within the timelines set out in this Agreement.

## Scope of Supply

Albrecht und Partner GmbH shall supply and deliver the following hardware items to Meridian Holdings Ltd under the terms of this Agreement:

- **Network Security Ap

eridian Holdings Ltd under the terms of this Agreement:

- **Network Security Appliance** — twelve (12) units at a unit price of twelve thousand five hundred pounds sterling (£12,500.00), giving a line total of one hundred and fifty thousand pounds sterling (£150,000.00) exclusive of applicable taxes.
- **Server Rack Unit 42U** — eight (8) units at a unit price of three thousand two hundred pounds

nit 42U** — eight (8) units at a unit price of three thousand two hundred pounds sterling (£3,200.00), giving a line total of twenty-five thousand six hundred pounds sterling (£25,600.00) exclusive of applicable taxes.

The aggregate hardware value under this Agreement is one hundred and seventy-five thousand six hundred pounds sterling (£175,600.00) exclusive of VAT. All hardware shall be deliver

ed pounds sterling (£175,600.00) exclusive of VAT. All hardware shall be delivered to the Buyer's primary data centre at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF, unless the Buyer designates an alternative delivery address in writing at least ten (10) business days before the scheduled delivery date.

The Supplier's nominated contract lead for this Agreement is Heinrich Müller, Managing Director of Albrecht und Partner GmbH.

All hardware shall be delivered to the Buyer's primary data centre at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF, unless the Buyer designates an alternative delivery address in writing at least ten (10) business days before the scheduled delivery date.

The Supplier's nominated contract lead for this Agreement is Heinrich Müller, Managing Director of Albrecht und Partner GmbH. Heinrich Müller holds authorit

, Managing Director of Albrecht und Partner GmbH. Heinrich Müller holds authority to confirm delivery schedules, approve substitutions, and execute change orders on behalf of the Supplier. The Buyer's nominated contract manager is James Blackwood, Chief Procurement Officer of Meridian Holdings Ltd. James Blackwood holds authority to accept deliveries, approve invoices, and authorise any changes to

James Blackwood holds authority to accept deliveries, approve invoices, and authorise any changes to the agreed specification in writing.

All hardware must be delivered with full manufacturer documentation, tested and certified to CE and UKCA marking requirements as applicable. The Supplier shall provide installation verification for each Network Security Appliance and rack assembly guidance for each Server Rack Unit 42U at no additional charge.

The Supplier shall provide installation verification for each Network Security Appliance and rack assembly guidance for each Server Rack Unit 42U at no additional charge. Legal matters relating to this Agreement are handled on behalf of the Supplier by Katarina Bauer, Legal Counsel at Albrecht und Partner GmbH.

## Payment Terms

The total contract value of one hundred and seventy-five thousand si

Legal matters relating to this Agreement are handled on behalf of the Supplier by Katarina Bauer, Legal Counsel at Albrecht und Partner GmbH.

## Payment Terms

The total contract value of one hundred and seventy-five thousand six hundred pounds sterling (£175,600.00) exclusive of VAT shall be invoiced and paid in two stages. The first invoice, covering fifty per cent (£87,800.00), shall be raised

stages. The first invoice, covering fifty per cent (£87,800.00), shall be raised by Albrecht und Partner GmbH on the date of despatch confirmation and is due within thirty (30) calendar days of receipt by Meridian Holdings Ltd. The second invoice, covering the remaining fifty per cent (£87,800.00), shall be raised following written acceptance of the full delivery by James Blackwood and is due with

The second invoice, covering the remaining fifty per cent (£87,800.00), shall be raised following written acceptance of the full delivery by James Blackwood and is due within thirty (30) calendar days of that invoice date.

All invoices must reference contract number K003 and be addressed to the accounts payable function of Meridian Holdings Ltd. Payment shall be made by BACS or international bank transfer to the account notified by Albrecht und Partner GmbH in writing.

Payment shall be made by BACS or international bank transfer to the account notified by Albrecht und Partner GmbH in writing. Overdue amounts shall accrue interest at three per cent (3%) per annum above the European Central Bank base rate, compounding monthly. Any invoice not formally disputed within ten (10) business days of receipt shall be deemed accepted.

## Liability

The Supplier's total ag

Any invoice not formally disputed within ten (10) business days of receipt shall be deemed accepted.

## Liability

The Supplier's total aggregate liability to the Buyer under or in connection with this Agreement, whether in contract, tort, or otherwise, shall not exceed the total fees paid by Meridian Holdings Ltd to Albrecht und Partner GmbH under this Agreement. Neither Party shall be liable to

Albrecht und Partner GmbH under this Agreement. Neither Party shall be liable to the other for indirect or consequential losses, including loss of revenue, loss of data, or loss of business opportunity. Nothing in this Agreement limits either Party's liability for fraud, death, or personal injury caused by negligence, or for product liability claims arising under applicable statute.

## Terminatio

Nothing in this Agreement limits either Party's liability for fraud, death, or personal injury caused by negligence, or for product liability claims arising under applicable statute.

## Termination

Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving thirty (30) calendar days' written notice to the other if no delivery has yet commenced. Once delivery of any item has commenced, termination shall not affect the Buyer's obligation to pay for hardware already despatched.

Once delivery of any item has commenced, termination shall not affect the Buyer's obligation to pay for hardware already despatched. Either Party may terminate immediately on written notice if the other commits a material breach and, where capable of remedy, fails to remedy that breach within twenty-one (21) calendar days of receiving written notice specifying the breach in reasonable detail. Meri

Either Party may terminate immediately on written notice if the other commits a material breach and, where capable of remedy, fails to remedy that breach within twenty-one (21) calendar days of receiving written notice specifying the breach in reasonable detail. Meridian Holdings Ltd may terminate with immediate effect if Albrecht und Partner GmbH enters insolvency proceedings or ceases to trade.

Meridian Holdings Ltd may terminate with immediate effect if Albrecht und Partner GmbH enters insolvency proceedings or ceases to trade.

## Governing Law

This Agreement, and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with it, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. Each Party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of

s. Each Party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

# Managed IT Services Agreement — Contract K004

**Effective Date:** 1 July 2025

**Parties:**

This Managed IT Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 1 July 2025 by and between:

**Meridian Holdings Ltd**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registration number 12847391, whose registered office is at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the Client"); and

**

ffice is at 45 King Street, London, EC2V 8RF (hereinafter "the Client"); and

**CentroLogic Industrie SA**, a company incorporated in France with registration number 75018324, whose registered office is at 15 Rue de la Paix, Paris, 75001, France (hereinafter "the Supplier").

Together the Client and the Supplier are referred to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

Meridian Holdings Ltd operates

ferred to as "the Parties."

---

## Background

Meridian Holdings Ltd operates a portfolio of UK business units that rely on continuous availability of on-premises server infrastructure, network systems, and licensed software applications. Following the completion of a cloud migration programme, the Client identified a requirement for structured managed services to maintain the retained on-premis

Following the completion of a cloud migration programme, the Client identified a requirement for structured managed services to maintain the retained on-premises estate and ensure all software licences remain current, compliant, and supported.

Following a competitive procurement exercise that included technical evaluation and a panel review, Meridian Holdings Ltd selected CentroLogic Industrie SA

tion and a panel review, Meridian Holdings Ltd selected CentroLogic Industrie SA on the basis of the Supplier's pan-European managed services capability, its demonstrable experience in multi-site infrastructure support, and its established framework for licence compliance management across enterprise environments.

## Scope of Services

CentroLogic Industrie SA shall provide the Managed IT Services Package to Meridian Holdings Ltd under the terms of this Agreement.

IT Services Package to Meridian Holdings Ltd under the terms of this Agreement. The Managed IT Services Package encompasses twenty-four-hour monitoring of the Client's on-premises server estate, proactive alerting for hardware and software failures, remote and on-site break-fix support, operating system patch management, configuration baseline management, and monthly infrastructure health reportin

The Managed IT Services Package encompasses twenty-four-hour monitoring of the Client's on-premises server estate, proactive alerting for hardware and software failures, remote and on-site break-fix support, operating system patch management, configuration baseline management, and monthly infrastructure health reporting.
